data.body:
PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness / anti-patterns / alternative-selection): mostly sound; one grounded mechanism-correctness finding on the canary's `computed > 0 && applied == 0` invariant vs. the dedup-skip idempotent re-sync case.

GROUNDING (live code):
- The silent no-op the plan targets is real and visible: `batch_dispatch.update_one` (src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/batch_dispatch.py:480-559) applies labels/comments/links in best-effort loops that LOG failures to stderr and DISCARD any count — only the scalar `result` dict is returned (line 561). bug 3f04 (lines 522-529) is exactly the link-drop the plan cites. Surfacing `*_applied` counts is the correct fix and is fitness-for-purpose.
- Parity target confirmed: inbound applier returns `links_applied` (apply_inbound.py:469/484/497) and the outbound batch path does not — the plan's premise holds.
- RECON line + structured outcome plumbing exists where the plan says (applier.py:753/779/819; reconcile.py:950/1044). Plumbing counts here is mechanically feasible.
- Approach selection: I generated an alternative (compute the canary in the differ/planning layer from the emitted sub-op set rather than the applier) — the plan's applier-layer placement is defensibly at-least-as-good (the applier is where applied!=computed actually diverges; the differ can't see runtime no-ops). Chosen approach is sound; alternatives discarded.
- Positive rationale present ("parity with inbound applier", single source of truth via story D, warn-first to avoid false-positive hard-fails). PASS on rationale.

FINDING (mechanism edge-case):
- [ ] Resolve the canary's false-positive on the LEGITIMATE dedup-skip path: in update_one the link loop SKIPS an add already present in either direction (batch_dispatch.py:550 "already present — no duplicate add"), so on an idempotent re-sync `computed > 0` (a link entry exists) but `applied == 0` even though nothing failed. The plan's invariant `computed > 0 && applied == 0` would fire a false positive there — precisely the kind of TOCTOU/idempotency edge the canary must not mistake for a silent failure.
  - criteria: G6
  - location: AC line "A test asserts that a mutation with computed sub-ops > 0 but applied == 0 fails" / plan "What" bullet 2 (`computed > 0 && applied == 0`)
  - evidence: batch_dispatch.py:543-551 (dedup skip), apply_inbound.py:466 (redundant-link guard makes re-apply idempotent)
  - suggested_fix: Define "applied" to include dedup-skipped-because-already-present (count a no-op-that-reached-desired-state as applied), OR scope the invariant to "computed > 0 && applied == 0 && deduped == 0". Add a canary test for the all-links-already-present re-sync asserting NO warning fires — this nails down the idempotent case the warn-first rollout would otherwise flag.

AFFIRMATIONS (G6): fitness-for-purpose (solves the named silent-no-op, not a proxy); positive rationale present; approach selection defensible vs. differ-layer alternative; warn-first rollout appropriately bounds blast radius of the new invariant.
